generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance ElectronicSpeedControllerActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Converts high-level speed commands into low-level motor voltage control signals for vehicle propulsion"
]

instance SteeringServoActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Executes steering commands received from the Digital Twin's control mechanisms"
]

instance MotorControllerActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Manages propulsion and braking actuation commands for the autonomous vehicle"
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Onboard LiDAR sensor for spatial mapping and environmental perception"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudData
]

instance IntelRealSenseD435Camera : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Depth camera providing 3D environmental perception for object recognition"
    DTDFVocab:producedData CameraDepthData
]

instance VehicleIMU : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Inertial Measurement Unit for motion tracking and navigation"
    DTDFVocab:producedData IMUStateData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Raw point cloud data from LiDAR sensors representing individual points on a 2D plane"
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR
]

instance CameraDepthData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "3D environmental perception data from depth camera for object recognition"
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IntelRealSenseD435Camera
]

instance IMUStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Inertial navigation and motion tracking data from vehicle IMU"
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleIMU
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Aggregated vehicle state information including speed, position, and actuation states"
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorControllerActuator, SteeringServoActuator, ElectronicSpeedControllerActuator
]

instance ObjectTracksData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Processed object detection and tracking data derived from raw LiDAR data"
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR
]

instance DrivingPathData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Projected driving trajectories generated from processed LiDAR data"
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR
]

instance ActuationFeedbackData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Processed feedback data from motor, steering, and servo actuation systems"
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorControllerActuator, SteeringServoActuator, ElectronicSpeedControllerActuator
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance PointCloudClusteringModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Software component performing real-time clustering and tracking of LiDAR point clouds"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Middleware
]

instance VehicleStateModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Digital representation of vehicle state including kinematics and dynamics"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Middleware
]

instance ACCScenarioSimulationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Virtual environment for replaying sensor data to simulate ACC scenarios"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Middleware
]

instance LiDARPointCloudDataProcessed : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Preprocessed LiDAR point cloud data after clustering and tracking"
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudClusteringModel
]

instance ProcessedObjectTracks : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured object detection and tracking data from raw LiDAR processing"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ObjectTracksData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Middleware
]

instance ProcessedDrivingPath : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Projected driving trajectories derived from processed LiDAR data"
    DTDFVocab:fromData DrivingPathData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Middleware
]

instance SynchronizedVehicleState : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time synchronized vehicle state data combining sensor and actuation feedback"
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateData, ActuationFeedbackData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VehicleStateModel
]

// C11: Enablers
instance ROS2Middleware : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Unified framework for integrating application modules and facilitating interoperability between physical and digital components"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance NVIDIAJetsonPlatform : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Edge computing platform providing real-time data processing and sensor fusion capabilities"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance MATLABSimulinkEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-based design environment for ACC system and digital twin logic development"
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance SQLDatabaseStorage : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Storage solution for synchronized twin-physical data enabling historical analysis"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance WiFiUDPProtocol : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Low-latency communication protocol for inter-entity data transmission"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time collection and transmission of sensor data (LiDAR, camera, IMU) from physical vehicle to Digital Twin for synchronization and analysis"
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeSensorDataInsight, BidirectionalSynchronizationAction
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Delegation of computationally intensive tasks (e.g., point cloud processing, trajectory planning) from embedded system to cloud/edge servers"
    DTDFVocab:provides ComputationalEfficiencyInsight, RemoteProcessingAction
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Centralized control and configuration of Digital Twin and physical system via remote interfaces"
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemConfigurationInsight, RemoteParameterAdjustmentAction
]

instance PointCloudProcessingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time processing of LiDAR point clouds for object detection and tracking"
    DTDFVocab:provides ObjectDetectionInsight, PathPlanningAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeSensorDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Continuous monitoring of synchronized sensor data streams from physical vehicle"
]

instance BidirectionalSynchronizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Maintains real-time synchronization between physical and virtual systems"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ComputationalEfficiencyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Analysis of computational load distribution between embedded and cloud systems"
]

instance RemoteProcessingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Delegates intensive computations to cloud/edge servers"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance SystemConfigurationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Current configuration state of Digital Twin and physical system parameters"
]

instance RemoteParameterAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Adjusts system parameters (e.g., ACC thresholds) without physical intervention"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ObjectDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time identification and tracking of objects in vehicle's environment"
]

instance PathPlanningAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Generates and adjusts driving trajectories based on object detection"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ACCScenarioSimulationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Validation results from replaying sensor data in virtual ACC scenarios"
]

// C1: Entities
instance PhysicalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Hosts resource-specific functionality including sensors, actuators, and compute resources for real-time data pre-processing and actuation commands"
]

instance DigitalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Centralizes data management and control mechanisms acting as core orchestration layer for twin coordination"
]

instance UserEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Contains User Interface Functional Entity with 3D Visualization and Control Panel subcomponents for human-machine interaction"
]

instance UserInterfaceFunctionalEntity : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Handles human-machine interaction without direct access to physical resources"
]

instance ThreeDVisualizationComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Real-time 3D visualization of processed data for operator monitoring"
]

instance ControlPanelComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Interface for remote management and configuration of the Digital Twin system"
]

// C5: Environments
instance AutonomousVehicleEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains PhysicalTwinEntity
]

instance DigitalTwinSimulationEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains DigitalTwinEntity, ACCScenarioSimulationModel
]

instance UserInteractionEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains UserEntity, ThreeDVisualizationComponent, ControlPanelComponent
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in this Digital Twin System (DTS) involves control flows from the Digital Twin Entity to the Physical Twin Entity, governed by the following technical specifications:

1. **Command Types**:
   - **Actuation Commands**: Generated by the **Actuation FE** component to control motor, steering, and servo systems.
   - **Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) Commands**: Issued by the **Controlling FE** component to regulate vehicle speed and distance based on processed LiDAR and vehicle state data.

2. **Control Message Formats**:
   - **Standardized via Data Translation**: All control messages are serialized and aligned with message types through the **Data Translation FE** component, ensuring compatibility across entities.
   - **ROS2 Message Formats**: Control commands are published as ROS2 messages within the internal SW network.

3. **Validation and Authentication Mechanisms**:
   - **Not explicitly detailed in the document**, but implied via the **Data Translation FE**’s role in ensuring message integrity and type alignment.
   - **Legacy System Integration**: The **Legacy Data Translation** component suggests compatibility checks for older systems, though specifics are not provided.

4. **Feedback Systems**:
   - **Actuation Feedback Loop**: Physical system responses (e.g., motor torque, steering angle) are fed back to the Digital Twin via the **Actuation FE** and **Vehicle State** data streams.
   - **Real-Time Visualization**: The **3D Visualization** component provides visual feedback (e.g., projected driving path, object tracks) to validate control decisions.

5. **Conflict Resolution**:
   - **Not explicitly described**, but the **Controlling FE** (ACC) likely implements priority-based resolution for conflicting commands (e.g., emergency braking vs. path adjustment).

6. **Command Execution Confirmation**:
   - **Implicit via Data Translation**: The **Data Translation FE** ensures command acknowledgment through message alignment and unit conversion, though explicit confirmation mechanisms (e.g., handshakes) are not detailed.
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin System (DTS) enforces a **100 ms clock synchronization accuracy** between physical and digital components to enable computation offloading and coordination, adhering to hardware limitations. This temporal granularity ensures deterministic behavior for safety-critical vehicular applications, where stricter synchronization (e.g., sub-100 ms) is mandated by standards but not achievable due to hardware constraints. The system leverages **time synchronization protocols** (unspecified but implied to be ROS2-compatible) to align distributed components across the **Physical Twin Entity**, **Digital Twin Entity**, and **User Entity**, ensuring real-time data consistency for services like *Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking* and actuation commands. Latency requirements are implicitly tied to the 100 ms synchronization window, as deviations could disrupt entity-based coordination (e.g., sensor-actuator loops or user interface responsiveness)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DTS orchestrates a **hybrid entity-based architecture** rooted in ISO 23247 RA but with **customized deviations** for automotive use cases. Key coordination mechanisms include:
  - **ROS2-based Component Integration**: Each **Functional Entity (FE)** is implemented as an **individual ROS2 package**, with nodes encapsulating specific functionalities (e.g., *Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking*). This enables **loose coupling** between entities while enforcing **message-passing synchronization** via ROS2 topics/services.
  - **Resource Management**: The **Physical Twin Entity** acts as the primary resource manager, hosting hardware-specific functionalities (sensors/actuators) and general automotive services. Its deviation from ISO 23247’s **OME (Object Management Entity)** highlights a shift toward **resource-centric orchestration**.
  - **System-Wide Coordination**: The **Digital Twin Entity** serves as the **control plane**, managing data flows between the Physical Twin (real-time data) and User Entity (visualization/control). Coordination is achieved via **ROS2’s distributed computing framework**, with **100 ms synchronization** ensuring temporal alignment across entities.
  - **Architecture Patterns**: The design follows an **event-driven, service-oriented pattern**, where FEs communicate asynchronously (e.g., sensor data → Digital Twin Entity → User Interface). The **absence of a centralized twin** reduces single points of failure but requires explicit **ownership definitions** for each FE’s functionality."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The **ISO 23247 standard (Part 2)** does not explicitly detail **fidelity requirements** (e.g., resolution, granularity, or abstraction levels) for the Digital Twin Entity (DTE). However, the **entity-based architecture** implies that fidelity is inherently tied to the **Observable Manufacturing Element (OME)**’s observable physical presence and operational data. **Validation and verification (V&V)** methods are not specified, but the standard’s **Data Collection and Device Control Entity** suggests a reliance on **real-time data synchronization** between the physical and digital domains. **Uncertainty sources** (e.g., sensor noise, model approximations) are not quantified, nor are **error detection/correction mechanisms** (e.g., anomaly detection, calibration protocols) described. **Quality assurance (QA)** processes are implied through the **User Entity’s** role in monitoring and controlling the OME, but no formal QA frameworks (e.g., traceability matrices, compliance checks) are referenced in the provided context."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **technical network infrastructure** for this Digital Twin System (DTS) is defined by the following specifications:

1. **Communication Protocols**:
   - **Internal SW Communication**: **ROS2 (Robot Operating System 2)** network for modular, interoperable communication between software components (e.g., **Data Pre-Processing FE**, **Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking**, **Controlling FE**).
   - **Inter-Entity Communication**: **Wi-Fi with UDP** at the transport layer for low-latency, high-throughput data exchange between the **Physical Twin Entity**, **Digital Twin Entity**, and **User Entity**.

2. **Network Topology and Architecture**:
   - **Hierarchical Model**:
     - **Physical Twin Entity**: Sensors (LiDAR, motor, steering, servo) feed data to the **Data Pre-Processing FE** and **Actuation FE**.
     - **Digital Twin Entity**: Hosts **Data Translation FE**, **Controlling FE**, **Reporting FE**, and **Presentation FE** components, interconnected via ROS2.
     - **User Entity**: Interfaces with the **3D Visualization** and **Control Panel** components for human-machine interaction.
   - **Data Flow Paths**: Sensor data propagates from Physical Twin → Digital Twin → User Entity (Fig. 2), with control signals flowing in reverse.

3. **Bandwidth and Latency Requirements**:
   - **Real-Time Constraints**: Critical for **LiDAR point cloud processing**, **object tracking**, and **ACC control**, implying **low-latency (<100ms)** and **high-bandwidth** requirements for UDP-based Wi-Fi communication.
   - **ROS2 Optimization**: Likely optimized for real-time performance within the internal SW network.

4. **Reliability and Redundancy Mechanisms**:
   - **Not explicitly detailed**, but ROS2 supports **reliable communication** via Quality of Service (QoS) policies (e.g., **RELIABLE** or **BEST_EFFORT** delivery).
   - **Data Translation FE**: Acts as a fail-safe for message serialization and unit conversion, reducing protocol mismatches.

5. **Security Protocols**:
   - **Not in Document**: No mention of encryption, authentication, or access control mechanisms for network communication.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **deployment infrastructure** for this Digital Twin System (DTS) includes the following technical and operational aspects:

1. **Hosting Platforms**:
   - **Edge Deployment**: Implied by the **on-board camera** and **LiDAR sensor** integration (Fig. 3), suggesting real-time processing at the **vehicle edge** to minimize latency.
   - **On-Premise/Embedded Systems**: The **Physical Twin Entity** (sensors and actuation systems) and **Digital Twin Entity** (SW components) are co-located or closely integrated, likely on embedded hardware (e.g., automotive ECUs or ROS2-compatible devices).

2. **Computational and Storage Requirements**:
   - **Computational**:
     - **Point Cloud Processing**: High computational demand for **LiDAR clustering and tracking** (e.g., GPU-accelerated algorithms).
     - **ROS2 Network**: Requires sufficient CPU/RAM for real-time message routing and component orchestration.
   - **Storage**:
     - **Data Storage FE**: Stores raw and processed data (e.g., LiDAR scans, reports) with unspecified capacity but likely optimized for **low-latency access**.
     - **Reporting FE**: Generates reports from aggregated data, implying structured storage (e.g., databases or log files).

3. **Scalability Mechanisms**:
   - **Modular ROS2 Architecture**: Components can be scaled independently (e.g., adding more LiDAR sensors or visualization nodes).
   - **Data Translation FE**: Acts as a bottleneck-free intermediary, supporting scalable data serialization.

4. **Deployment Models Supported**:
   - **Hybrid Edge-Cloud**: While primarily edge-deployed (vehicle-onboard), the **Wi-Fi UDP** inter-entity communication suggests potential for **cloud-offloading** of non-real-time tasks (e.g., report generation).
   - **Legacy Integration**: The **Legacy Data Translation** component indicates support for integrating older systems (e.g., non-ROS2 hardware).

5. **Availability and Reliability Assurance**:
   - **ROS2 Redundancy**: Built-in support for **component redundancy** and **fault tolerance** via ROS2’s service and action frameworks.
   - **Real-Time Constraints**: Critical for **ACC and actuation**, implying **high-availability** mechanisms (e.g., watchdog timers, failover protocols) though not explicitly detailed.
   - **Not in Document**: No mention of backup systems, disaster recovery, or SLA compliance.
"
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adheres to the **ISO 23247 – Digital twin framework for manufacturing**, specifically **Part 2: Reference architecture**, which defines an **entity-based reference architecture** for DT implementation. Key compliance aspects include:
  - **Entity-based modularity**: The system is structured around **Observable Manufacturing Element (OME)**, **Data Collection and Device Control Entity**, **Digital Twin Entity**, and **User Entity**, each with defined responsibilities.
  - **Interoperability standards**: The framework allows flexibility in implementation (e.g., **OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA)** for data exchange) but does not mandate specific protocols beyond entity-level communication.
  - **Certification requirements**: Not explicitly detailed in the provided context.
  - **Compliance frameworks**: The ISO 23247 standard ensures alignment with **manufacturing-specific DT principles**, including **data representation**, **information exchange**, and **user interaction** as per Parts 1, 3, and 4 of the standard.
  The architecture emphasizes **standardized interfaces** between entities to ensure **scalability** and **interoperability** across manufacturing systems."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adheres to **ISO 15622:2018** for **Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) performance requirements**, including real-time data collection and processing to ensure safe vehicle operation. The system incorporates **Vehicle-to-Vehicle (V2V) communication** as specified in **3GPP TS 22.185 (Release 15)**, which mandates low-latency communication (≤100 ms for critical safety messages) to prevent collisions and maintain situational awareness. However, the provided documents do not explicitly detail **cybersecurity protocols**, **access control mechanisms**, or **authentication systems** for the Digital Twin. The system does not reference **ISO 26262-1:2018** for functional safety, nor does it specify **fail-safe mechanisms** or **risk assessment methodologies** beyond the scope of ACC and V2V communication."
]
